ucs.title: [LLM] Sowbug (G0054) diplomatic file-share enumeration via cmd.exe with foreign-policy keyword filters
ucs.description: Hunts the signature Sowbug tradecraft documented by Symantec (Nov 2017): operators running cmd.exe / batch scripts against mapped network shares of South American and South-East Asian foreign ministries to recursively `dir` for documents matching diplomatic / regional keywords (e.g. *foreign*, *NATO*, *ASEAN*, country names) before staging them. The combination of cmd.exe + recursive directory listing + diplomatic-string filter is highly idiosyncratic to Sowbug and rarely seen from legitimate users.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdlines values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parents from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name="cmd.exe" AND (Processes.process="*dir *" OR Processes.process="*net view*" OR Processes.process="*net use*" OR Processes.process="*xcopy*" OR Processes.process="*forfiles*") AND (Processes.process="*foreign*" OR Processes.process="*ministry*" OR Processes.process="*embass*" OR Processes.process="*nato*" OR Processes.process="*asean*" OR Processes.process="*policy*" OR Processes.process="*diplomat*" OR Processes.process="*argentin*" OR Processes.process="*brazil*" OR Processes.process="*ecuador*" OR Processes.process="*peru*" OR Processes.process="*malays*" OR Processes.process="*indones*" OR Processes.process="*vietnam*" OR Processes.process="*singapor*") AND (Processes.process="*\\\\*" OR Processes.process="*/s*" OR Processes.process="* /s *") AND NOT Processes.user IN ("*$","SYSTEM","LOCAL SERVICE","NETWORK SERVICE") by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.parent_process_name | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime)
ucs.kql:
// Sowbug (G0054) — foreign-policy keyword enumeration on network shares
let _diplomatic_terms = dynamic(["foreign","ministry","embass","nato","asean","policy","diplomat","argentin","brazil","ecuador","peru","malays","indones","vietnam","singapor","recen"]);
let _enum_verbs       = dynamic(["dir ","net view","net use","xcopy","forfiles","robocopy","copy "]);
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where AccountName !endswith "$"             // skip machine accounts
| where FileName =~ "cmd.exe" or InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "cmd.exe"
| extend Cmd = tolower(ProcessCommandLine)
| where Cmd has_any (_enum_verbs)
| where Cmd has "\\\\" or Cmd has " /s"     // UNC path or recursive listing
| where Cmd has_any (_diplomatic_terms)
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName,
          ProcessCommandLine,
          Parent    = InitiatingProcessFileName,
          ParentCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          IsRemoteSession = InitiatingProcessAccountName
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// Sowbug (G0054) — foreign-policy keyword enumeration on network shares — 4688 path
let _diplomatic_terms = dynamic(["foreign","ministry","embass","nato","asean","policy","diplomat","argentin","brazil","ecuador","peru","malays","indones","vietnam","singapor","recen"]);
let _enum_verbs       = dynamic(["dir ","net view","net use","xcopy","forfiles","robocopy","copy "]);
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where EventID == 4688
| where Account !endswith "$"
| where NewProcessName endswith "\\cmd.exe" or ParentProcessName endswith "\\cmd.exe"
| extend Cmd = tolower(CommandLine)
| where Cmd has_any (_enum_verbs)
| where Cmd has "\\\\" or Cmd has " /s"
| where Cmd has_any (_diplomatic_terms)
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, NewProcessName, CommandLine, ParentProcessName
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Sowbug Diplomatic Network Share Enumeration via cmd.exe
id: 7b2c1a4d-9f1e-4a4f-8a18-6cf7b16f9c11
status: experimental
description: Detects Sowbug (G0054) tradecraft of running cmd.exe against network shares with recursive dir / net view / xcopy filtered by diplomatic / regional keywords.
references:
    - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0054/
    - https://www.symantec.com/connect/blogs/sowbug-cyber-espionage-group-targets-south-american-and-southeast-asian-governments
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/03
tags:
    - attack.discovery
    - attack.collection
    - attack.t1059.003
    - attack.t1135
    - attack.t1083
    - attack.t1039
    - attack.g0054
logsource:
    category: process_creation
    product: windows
detection:
    selection_proc:
        Image|endswith: '\cmd.exe'
    selection_enum:
        CommandLine|contains:
            - 'dir '
            - 'net view'
            - 'net use'
            - 'xcopy'
            - 'forfiles'
            - 'robocopy'
    selection_share_or_recursive:
        CommandLine|contains:
            - '\\\\'
            - ' /s'
    selection_diplomatic:
        CommandLine|contains:
            - 'foreign'
            - 'ministry'
            - 'embass'
            - 'nato'
            - 'asean'
            - 'policy'
            - 'diplomat'
            - 'argentin'
            - 'brazil'
            - 'ecuador'
            - 'peru'
            - 'malays'
            - 'indones'
            - 'vietnam'
            - 'singapor'
    filter_machine:
        User|endswith: '$'
    condition: all of selection_* and not filter_machine
falsepositives:
    - Legitimate analysts running keyword-based document searches on file servers
    - Auditors performing data-classification scans
level: high
ucs.rationale: Sowbug is one of the few tracked groups whose published tradecraft (Symantec, Nov 2017) specifically describes operators running cmd.exe scripts against mapped diplomatic file shares with regex/keyword filters tuned to foreign-policy documents in Latin America and SE Asia. Random APT crews dump shares wholesale; Sowbug filters at collection time. The triple-predicate (cmd.exe + recursive/UNC + dip

ucs.title: [LLM] Sowbug (G0054) Felismus loader — Windows-binary masquerade in user-writable paths followed by RAR staging
ucs.description: Hunts Sowbug's Felismus deployment pattern documented by Symantec: dropping binaries with legitimate-looking Windows / Adobe / Symantec names (e.g. adobecms.exe, ws-trust.exe, fb.exe) into non-system, user-writable paths, then staging stolen documents with rar.exe (often renamed) prior to exfil. Pairs T1036.005 masquerading with T1560.001 archive-collected-data — a tight Sowbug-specific chain.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdlines values(Processes.process_path) as paths from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.process_name IN ("adobecms.exe","ws-trust.exe","fb.exe","WindowsUpdate.exe","wsmprovhost.exe","symantec.exe") OR (Processes.process_name="rar.exe" OR Processes.process="* a -hp* *" OR Processes.process="* a -r -m5 *" OR Processes.process="*-hp* *.rar*")) AND NOT (Processes.process_path IN ("*\\Windows\\System32\\*","*\\Windows\\SysWOW64\\*","*\\Program Files\\*","*\\Program Files (x86)\\*")) AND (Processes.process_path="*\\Users\\*" OR Processes.process_path="*\\ProgramData\\*" OR Processes.process_path="*\\Windows\\Debug\\*" OR Processes.process_path="*\\PerfLogs\\*" OR Processes.process_path="*\\Temp\\*") by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.process_name Processes.process_path Processes.parent_process_name | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime) | where firstTime!=""
ucs.kql:
// Sowbug (G0054) — Felismus-style masquerade + RAR staging
let _sowbug_names = dynamic(["adobecms.exe","ws-trust.exe","fb.exe","windowsupdate.exe","wsmprovhost.exe","symantec.exe"]);
let _archive_bins = dynamic(["rar.exe","winrar.exe","7z.exe","7za.exe"]);
let _legit_paths  = dynamic([@"c:\windows\system32\", @"c:\windows\syswow64\", @"c:\program files\", @"c:\program files (x86)\"]);
let _suspect_paths = dynamic([@"\users\", @"\programdata\", @"\windows\debug\", @"\perflogs\", @"\temp\", @"\appdata\local\temp\"]);
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
| extend FileLower = tolower(FileName), PathLower = tolower(FolderPath), CmdLower = tolower(ProcessCommandLine)
| where (FileLower in (_sowbug_names))
     or (FileLower in (_archive_bins))
     or (CmdLower matches regex @"\s+a\s+-(hp|r|m5)\b")   // RAR archive flags incl. password
| where not(PathLower has_any (_legit_paths))
| where PathLower has_any (_suspect_paths)
| join kind=leftouter (
    DeviceFileEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | where ActionType == "FileCreated"
    | where FileName endswith ".rar" or FileName endswith ".7z" or FileName endswith ".zip"
    | project DeviceId, ArchiveCreated = Timestamp, ArchiveName = FileName, ArchivePath = FolderPath
  ) on DeviceId
| where isnull(ArchiveCreated) or (ArchiveCreated between (Timestamp - 30m .. Timestamp + 30m))
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, FileName, FolderPath,
          ProcessCommandLine, SHA256,
          ParentImage = InitiatingProcessFolderPath,
          ParentCmd   = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          ArchiveCreated, ArchiveName, ArchivePath
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// Sowbug (G0054) — Felismus masquerade + RAR staging — 4688 path
let _sowbug_names = dynamic(["adobecms.exe","ws-trust.exe","fb.exe","windowsupdate.exe","wsmprovhost.exe","symantec.exe"]);
let _archive_bins = dynamic(["rar.exe","winrar.exe","7z.exe","7za.exe"]);
let _legit_paths  = dynamic([@"c:\windows\system32\", @"c:\windows\syswow64\", @"c:\program files\", @"c:\program files (x86)\"]);
let _suspect_paths = dynamic([@"\users\", @"\programdata\", @"\windows\debug\", @"\perflogs\", @"\temp\", @"\appdata\local\temp\"]);
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where EventID == 4688
| where Account !endswith "$"
| extend ProcLower = tolower(NewProcessName), CmdLower = tolower(CommandLine)
| extend Leaf = tostring(split(ProcLower, "\\")[-1])
| where (Leaf in (_sowbug_names))
     or (Leaf in (_archive_bins))
     or (CmdLower matches regex @"\s+a\s+-(hp|r|m5)\b")
| where not(ProcLower has_any (_legit_paths))
| where ProcLower has_any (_suspect_paths)
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, NewProcessName, CommandLine, ParentProcessName, TokenElevationType
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.rationale: The Symantec writeup that established the Sowbug cluster called out exactly this artefact set — Felismus components named to mimic Adobe / Symantec / Windows Update binaries, dropped in user-writable paths (never under \Windows\System32\), used in conjunction with rar.exe staging of the diplomatic documents harvested via UC1. Treating the masquerade names + non-canonical path + nearby archive crea
